segments.text: At this my heart also pounds and leaps from its place.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Listen closely to the thunder of His voice and the rumbling that comes from His mouth.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He unleashes His lightning beneath the whole sky and sends it to the ends of the earth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then there comes a roaring sound; He thunders with His majestic voice. He does not restrain the lightningwhen His voice resounds.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God thunders wondrously with His voice; He does great things we cannot comprehend.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For He says to the snow, ‘Fall on the earth,’and to the gentle rain, ‘Pour out a mighty downpour.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He seals up the hand of every man, so that all men may know His work.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The wild animals enter their lairs; they settle down in their dens.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The tempest comes from its chamber, and the cold from the driving north winds.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: By the breath of God the ice is formed and the watery expanses are frozen.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He loads the clouds with moisture; He scatters His lightning through them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They swirl about, whirling at His direction, accomplishing all that He commandsover the face of all the earth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Whether for punishment or for His land, He accomplishes this in His loving devotion.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Listen to this, O Job; st and still and consider the wonders of God.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Do you know how God dispatches the cloudsor makes the lightning flash?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Do you understand how the clouds float, those wonders of Him who is perfect in knowledge?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: You whose clothes get hotwhen the land lies hushed under the south wind,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: can you, like Him, spread out the skiesto reflect the heat like a mirror of bronze?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Teach us what we should say to Him; we cannot draw up our case when our faces are in darkness.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Should He be told that I want to speak? Would a man ask to be swallowed up?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text:
Now no one can gaze at the sunwhen it is bright in the skies
after the wind has swept them clean.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Out of the north He comes in golden splendor; awesome majesty surrounds Him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The Almighty is beyond our reach; He is exalted in power! In His justice and great righteousnessHe does not oppress.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore, men fear Him, for He is not partial to the wise in heart.
segments.speaker: Unknown
